segments.text: First listen, and then answer the question.

translations.zh-Hans: 先听录音，然后回答问题。

segments.text: What two interruptions did the writer have?

translations.zh-Hans: 作者两次被什么事情打断？

segments.text: After breakfast, I sent the children to school, and then I went to the shops.

translations.zh-Hans: 早饭后，我送孩子们上学，然后去商店买东西。

segments.text: It was still early when I returned home.
The children were at school, my husband was at work, and the house was quiet.

translations.zh-Hans: 孩子们在学校，丈夫在上班，屋里静悄悄的。

segments.text: So I decided to make some meat pies.

translations.zh-Hans: 于是我决定做些肉馅饼。

segments.text: In a short time, I was busy mixing butter and flour, and my hands were soon covered with sticky pastry.

translations.zh-Hans: 不一会儿，我就忙着搅拌黄油和面粉，双手很快沾满了黏糊糊的面团。

segments.text: At exactly that moment, the telephone rang.

translations.zh-Hans: 恰好就在这时，电话响了。

segments.text: Nothing could have been more annoying.

translations.zh-Hans: 没有比这更烦人的事了。

segments.text: I picked up the receiver between two sticky fingers and was dismayed when I recognized the voice of Helen Bates.

translations.zh-Hans: 我用两根黏糊糊的手指夹起听筒，认出是海伦·贝茨的声音时感到很沮丧。

segments.text: It took me ten minutes to persuade her to ring back later.

translations.zh-Hans: 我花了十分钟才说服她晚些时候再打来。

segments.text: At last, I hung up the receiver.
There was pastry on my fingers, on the telephone, and on the doorknobs.

translations.zh-Hans: 我的手指、电话和门把手上全是面团。

segments.text: I had no sooner got back to the kitchen than the doorbell rang loud enough to wake the dead.

translations.zh-Hans: 我刚回到厨房，门铃就大声响了起来，足以把死人吵醒。

segments.text: This time it was the postman, and he wanted me to sign for a registered letter.

translations.zh-Hans: 这次是邮递员，他要我签收一封挂号信。
